question_text: Which statement correctly describes gas pressure?

options.label: A
options.text: Gas pressure is caused by particles pushing down due to gravity

options.label: B
options.text: Gas pressure is caused by particles colliding with the container walls

options.label: C
options.text: Gas pressure only exists in hot gases

options.label: D
options.text: Gas pressure decreases when particles move faster

correct_answer: Gas pressure is caused by particles colliding with the container walls
explanation: Gas pressure is created when gas particles move randomly and constantly bump into the walls of their container. Each collision exerts a small force, and the total effect of millions of these collisions produces the pressure we measure.
